title: Weighted-coin almost-luck
text: A coin habit that slightly biases trivial chance; never casinos, never life-or-death until ranked up.

title: Soft voice throw
text: Throw a whisper a few meters; crowded rooms garble it into comedy.

title: Pain-box compartment
text: Temporarily shelf pain into later; bill comes due with interest.

title: Tool-echo improvisation
text: Treat a wrong tool as almost-right for one task; breaks more often than it works early on.

title: Face-blur in memory
text: People struggle to describe your face after brief meetings; cameras are less impressed.

title: Click-echo room sense
text: Click tongue to roughly map a dark room; noisy places defeat it.

title: Clause-catch reading
text: Spot one predatory clause in a contract; legalese still swims.

title: Pocket seed sprout
text: Force a seed to sprout early in poor soil; plant may hate you for it.

title: Spark-gap static jump
text: Jump a tiny spark between metal points; useful for lights, bad for friends.

title: Dream-tag recall
text: Wake with one true tag from a dream about a real place; usually useless, rarely perfect.

title: Pack-balance instinct
text: Sense when a load will tip a cart or person; ignores emotional baggage.

title: Market half-haggle
text: Shave a little price with patience; prideful sellers dig in harder.

title: Poison-sip caution
text: Detect harsh adulterants by micro-sip risk; still stupid without training.

title: Mirror-form copy
text: Copy a simple physical motion after seeing it once; complex arts need ranks.
examples: copy the knot, fail the flourish

title: Quiet-door hinge feel
text: Feel which doors squeak before opening; oil is still your friend.

title: Ink-smudge forgery dab
text: Smudge a seal almost convincingly; experts laugh until your rank rises.

title: Pulse-count calm
text: Count pulse to steady hands for one precise action; panic breaks the count.

title: Softcap until mentor
text: Practice XP halves after mid-rank until a mentor, setback, or breakthrough event.

title: Three-tier risk XP
text: Safe practice times one, contested times two, life-risk times three; life-risk failure can injure.

title: Reputation-gated XP
text: Some ranks need public proof or face, not just hours; shame blocks progress.

title: Material sink ranks
text: Rank-ups consume rare materials or cash; talent alone stalls.

title: Seasonal slow tick
text: Skills tick up slightly each season of relevant work; fast spikes need crisis.

title: Fail-forward insight XP
text: Meaningful failures grant more insight XP than easy wins; empty grind grants near nothing.

title: Pair-training bonus
text: Training with a rival or partner multiplies XP; alone practice is honest and slow.

title: Discover-on-use skills
text: Skill names appear only after repeated use or teaching; no pre-bought trees at start.

title: Mentor-gated specialties
text: Specialties require a person, manual, or institution, not pure self-study.

title: Single-domain focus
text: One domain deep beats many shallow; second combat kit blocked at start.

title: Social ladder ranks
text: Etiquette and face ranks open quests more than stealth ranks in court-heavy worlds.
examples: rank B enters the inner hall

title: Craft quality steps
text: Craft ranks convert to item quality steps with failure risk and material loss.

title: System node real cost
text: UI skill nodes cost stamina, money, or story flags, not free points only.

title: Language rank doors
text: Language ranks open markets, courts, and secrets; wrong rank is comedy or insult.

title: No window, only omens
text: Progress is tracked by mentors, scars, and omens with no blue panes.

title: Full RPG chrome UI
text: Levels, inventories, and damage numbers are diegetic and contested by other systems.

title: Profession ladder only
text: Levels are job ranks (dockhand to clerk), not murder XP.

title: Honor point ledger
text: A visible honor or face score that gates housing, weapons, and apologies.

title: Heat meter crime
text: Crime raises heat that changes patrols, prices, and who will talk to you.
